channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UK Health Secretary Resigns Amid Leadership Challenge Against Starmer
item.description: The political scene in the United Kingdom has taken a dramatic turn as Health Secretary Wes Streeting announced his resignation from Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Cabinet. This move marks the first ma...

item.title: Delhi Government Announces Fuel Conservation Measures Following PM Modi's Appeal
item.description: In response to Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s call for fuel conservation, Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta announced a comprehensive set of measures aimed at reducing energy consumption and promoting ...

item.title: Brigitte Bauer: Iconic Model and Fashion Visionary of the 1960s
item.description: Brigitte Bauer was a German-born fashion model who became an iconic figure of 1960s fashion, symbolizing the era's shift from classic elegance to the playful, energetic 'Youthquake' movement. Discover...

item.title: Summit Highlights Comprehensive Approaches to Maternal and Newborn Care in India
item.description: The Times Future of Maternity 2026 (TFM 2026) summit in New Delhi focused on advancing maternal and newborn health in India, moving beyond survival to promoting healthier generations. The event, organ...

item.title: Tanzania Must Reduce Human Reliance in Local Revenue Collection to Fight Leakages
item.description: The article by Mariam Gichan highlights big challenges in how local councils in Tanzania collect revenue. Most collections still depend heavily on human workers who move from market to market, bus sto...

item.title: Poverty Forces Many Young People in Mahonda, Zanzibar to Leave School
item.description: In Mahonda, North Unguja, Zanzibar, the visible signs of development—like new houses, tarred roads, and shops—mask a harsh reality. Every year, around 300 young people are leaving school because of ex...

item.title: Majority of ODM Supporters Prefer Sifuna's Linda Mwananchi Movement, Says TIFA Poll
item.description: A recent TIFA Research opinion poll has shown that a significant majority of ODM supporters in Kenya prefer the Linda Mwananchi movement led by Nairobi Senator Edwin Sifuna over Senator Oburu Odinga’s...

item.title: Single 6D Lotto Winner Claims P8.676 Million Prize
item.description: A lucky individual struck it rich in the 6D Lotto draw on May 14, 2026, according to the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO). The winning combination, 4-3-4-7-2-0, awarded the bettor a prize ...

item.title: Western Cape Floods Leave 10 Dead as Rescue Efforts Continue
item.description: The Western Cape is still grappling with the aftermath of severe storms, even though the weather has improved in some areas. The provincial government reports that emergency response and recovery oper...

item.title: Tanzania Launches Special Mining Zones and Financial Support for Youth Investors
item.description: The Government of Tanzania is making strong moves to involve young people in the mining sector for the 2026/2027 financial year. The Tanzania Mining Commission has set aside 65 special mining areas ac...

item.title: Legal Expert Criticizes Alberta Judge's Decision to Quash Secession Petition
item.description: In a recent Alberta Court of King’s Bench ruling, a trial judge invalidated a petition seeking a referendum on Alberta's potential secession, which had garnered over 300,000 signatures. Dwight Newman,...

item.title: ACT public schools to close for two-hour strike over staff working conditions
item.description: All public schools in the Australian Capital Territory (ACT) will close for two hours on Friday, May 22, due to a planned strike by teachers and school staff protesting the conditions outlined in a pr...

item.title: Court orders Zuma and Thales to proceed with arms deal trial despite delay tactics
item.description: Former president Jacob Zuma and French arms company Thales have been instructed by the Pietermaritzburg High Court to cease employing what are known as 'Stalingrad tactics'—a legal strategy involving ...

item.title: Senate Has No Authority to Place Bato Under Protective Custody, Says Sergeant-at-Arms
item.description: The Sergeant-at-Arms of the Philippine Senate clarified that the Senate does not have the authority to impose protective custody on former law enforcement official Bato. This statement comes amid publ...

item.title: Trump invests heavily in Kentucky primary against Rep. Massie amid voter resistance
item.description: President Donald Trump is heavily targeting Rep. Thomas Massie in the Kentucky Republican primary, spending over $29 million on advertising alone. This makes it one of the most expensive primaries in ...

item.title: Why South Africa Should Include Boys in Its HPV Vaccination Programme
item.description: In South Africa, the government provides free HPV vaccinations to girls aged nine to 14 through school programmes to prevent cervical cancer, but boys are currently excluded from this public initiativ...

item.title: Mbavu Destroyer Pulls Out of Vurugu II, Focuses on Personal Mission
item.description: Kenya's boxing star, Mbavu Destroyer, has officially announced that he won’t be taking part in the upcoming Vurugu II showdown set for June. The news came through a video he shared while walking out o...

item.title: Photographer Maurice Wolf Captures Traditional Shepherd Migration in Georgia
item.description: Maurice Wolf, an Australian-Dutch photographer, documented the centuries-old practice of transhumance in the highlands of Tusheti, Georgia. This seasonal migration involves shepherds guiding their flo...

item.title: INEC Plans Major NYSC Deployment for 2027 Elections
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) don announce say dem go deploy no fewer than 1.4 million National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) members for the 2027 general elections. Prof Joash Amu...

item.title: A.K. Antony advises V.D. Satheesan to take time before addressing Kerala's financial crisis
item.description: V.D. Satheesan, the Chief Minister-designate of Kerala, met senior Congress leaders A.K. Antony and V.M. Sudheeran on May 14, 2026. During the meeting, Mr. Antony urged the public to give Mr. Satheesa...
